<content>1: package com.shiftworks.jobops.service;
2: 
3: import com.shiftworks.jobops.dto.AppealCreateRequest;
4: import com.shiftworks.jobops.dto.AppealProcessRequest;
5: import com.shiftworks.jobops.entity.Appeal;
6: import com.shiftworks.jobops.entity.JobPosting;
7: import com.shiftworks.jobops.entity.User;
8: import com.shiftworks.jobops.enums.AppealStatus;
9: import com.shiftworks.jobops.enums.JobStatus;
10: import com.shiftworks.jobops.enums.UserRole;
11: import com.shiftworks.jobops.exception.BusinessException;
12: import com.shiftworks.jobops.repository.AppealRepository;
13: import com.shiftworks.jobops.repository.JobPostingRepository;
14: import com.shiftworks.jobops.repository.ReviewActionRepository;
15: import com.shiftworks.jobops.repository.UserRepository;
16: import com.shiftworks.jobops.security.AuthenticatedUser;
17: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;
18: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
19: import org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;
20: import org.mockito.InjectMocks;
21: import org.mockito.Mock;
22: import org.mockito.junit.jupiter.MockitoExtension;
23: import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
24: 
25: import java.util.Optional;
26: 
27: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
28: import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;
29: import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq;
30: import static org.mockito.Mockito.*;
31: 
32: @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
33: class AppealServiceTest {
34: 
35:     @Mock private AppealRepository appealRepository;
36:     @Mock private JobPostingRepository jobPostingRepository;
37:     @Mock private ReviewActionRepository reviewActionRepository;
38:     @Mock private UserRepository userRepository;
39:     @Mock private JobHistoryService jobHistoryService;
40:     @Mock private AuditService auditService;
41:     @InjectMocks private AppealService appealService;
42: 
43:     private AuthenticatedUser employerUser;
44:     private AuthenticatedUser reviewerUser;
45:     private User employerEntity;
46:     private User reviewerEntity;
47: 
48:     @BeforeEach
49:     void setup() {
50:         employerUser = new AuthenticatedUser(1L, "employer1", UserRole.EMPLOYER);
51:         reviewerUser = new AuthenticatedUser(2L, "reviewer1", UserRole.REVIEWER);
52:         employerEntity = new User();
53:         employerEntity.setId(1L);
54:         employerEntity.setUsername("employer1");
55:         employerEntity.setRole(UserRole.EMPLOYER);
56:         reviewerEntity = new User();
57:         reviewerEntity.setId(2L);
58:         reviewerEntity.setUsername("reviewer1");
59:         reviewerEntity.setRole(UserRole.REVIEWER);
60:     }
61: 
62:     private JobPosting buildJob(JobStatus status) {
63:         JobPosting job = new JobPosting();
64:         job.setId(10L);
65:         job.setStatus(status);
66:         job.setEmployer(employerEntity);
67:         return job;
68:     }
69: 
70:     private Appeal buildAppeal(JobPosting job) {
71:         Appeal appeal = new Appeal();
72:         appeal.setId(100L);
73:         appeal.setJobPosting(job);
74:         appeal.setEmployer(employerEntity);
75:         appeal.setAppealReason("This was wrongly taken down and I want it reinstated");
76:         appeal.setStatus(AppealStatus.PENDING);
77:         return appeal;
78:     }
79: 
80:     @Test
81:     void createAppealSuccess() {
82:         JobPosting job = buildJob(JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN);
83:         when(jobPostingRepository.findByIdAndEmployer_Id(10L, 1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(job));
84:         when(appealRepository.existsByJobPosting_IdAndStatus(10L, AppealStatus.PENDING)).thenReturn(false);
85: 
86:         AppealCreateRequest request = new AppealCreateRequest(10L, "This was wrongly taken down and I want it reinstated");
87:         appealService.createAppeal(request, employerUser);
88: 
89:         verify(appealRepository).save(any(Appeal.class));
90:         assertEquals(JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING, job.getStatus());
91:         verify(jobPostingRepository).save(job);
92:     }
93: 
94:     @Test
95:     void createAppealFailsIfNotTakenDown() {
96:         JobPosting job = buildJob(JobStatus.PUBLISHED);
97:         when(jobPostingRepository.findByIdAndEmployer_Id(10L, 1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(job));
98: 
99:         AppealCreateRequest request = new AppealCreateRequest(10L, "This was wrongly taken down and I want it reinstated");
100:         var ex = assertThrows(BusinessException.class, () -> appealService.createAppeal(request, employerUser));
101:         assertEquals(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, ex.getStatus());
102:     }
103: 
104:     @Test
105:     void createAppealFailsIfPendingExists() {
106:         JobPosting job = buildJob(JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN);
107:         when(jobPostingRepository.findByIdAndEmployer_Id(10L, 1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(job));
108:         when(appealRepository.existsByJobPosting_IdAndStatus(10L, AppealStatus.PENDING)).thenReturn(true);
109: 
110:         AppealCreateRequest request = new AppealCreateRequest(10L, "This was wrongly taken down and I want it reinstated");
111:         var ex = assertThrows(BusinessException.class, () -> appealService.createAppeal(request, employerUser));
112:         assertEquals(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, ex.getStatus());
113:     }
114: 
115:     @Test
116:     void createAppealForbiddenForReviewer() {
117:         AppealCreateRequest request = new AppealCreateRequest(10L, "This was wrongly taken down and I want it reinstated");
118:         var ex = assertThrows(BusinessException.class, () -> appealService.createAppeal(request, reviewerUser));
119:         assertEquals(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, ex.getStatus());
120:     }
121: 
122:     @Test
123:     void processAppealGranted() {
124:         JobPosting job = buildJob(JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING);
125:         Appeal appeal = buildAppeal(job);
126:         when(appealRepository.findById(100L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(appeal));
127:         when(userRepository.findById(2L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(reviewerEntity));
128: 
129:         AppealProcessRequest request = new AppealProcessRequest("GRANTED", "The takedown was indeed wrong, reinstating");
130:         appealService.processAppeal(100L, request, reviewerUser);
131: 
132:         assertEquals(AppealStatus.GRANTED, appeal.getStatus());
133:         assertEquals(JobStatus.PUBLISHED, job.getStatus());
134:         verify(appealRepository).save(appeal);
135:     }
136: 
137:     @Test
138:     void processAppealDenied() {
139:         JobPosting job = buildJob(JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING);
140:         Appeal appeal = buildAppeal(job);
141:         when(appealRepository.findById(100L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(appeal));
142:         when(userRepository.findById(2L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(reviewerEntity));
143: 
144:         AppealProcessRequest request = new AppealProcessRequest("DENIED", "The takedown was correct, violation confirmed");
145:         appealService.processAppeal(100L, request, reviewerUser);
146: 
147:         assertEquals(AppealStatus.DENIED, appeal.getStatus());
148:         assertEquals(JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN, job.getStatus());
149:     }
150: 
151:     @Test
152:     void processAppealForbiddenForEmployer() {
153:         AppealProcessRequest request = new AppealProcessRequest("GRANTED", "The takedown was indeed wrong, reinstating");
154:         var ex = assertThrows(BusinessException.class, () -> appealService.processAppeal(100L, request, employerUser));
155:         assertEquals(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, ex.getStatus());
156:     }
157: 
158:     @Test
159:     void processAlreadyProcessedAppealFails() {
160:         JobPosting job = buildJob(JobStatus.PUBLISHED);
161:         Appeal appeal = buildAppeal(job);
162:         appeal.setStatus(AppealStatus.GRANTED);
163:         when(appealRepository.findById(100L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(appeal));
164:         when(userRepository.findById(2L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(reviewerEntity));
165: 
166:         AppealProcessRequest request = new AppealProcessRequest("DENIED", "Trying to re-process this appeal after grant");
167:         var ex = assertThrows(BusinessException.class, () -> appealService.processAppeal(100L, request, reviewerUser));
168:         assertEquals(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, ex.getStatus());
169:     }
170: }

content.text:
**Summarizing test additions and issues**

I'll explain the addition of the AppealServiceTest to cover creation and processing flows, noting the test command attempted in the backend directory failed due to missing Maven (`mvn` command not found and no wrapper). I'll suggest next steps like installing Maven or running tests once it's available, and carefully reference the test file path without committing anything.

New `AppealServiceTest` covers every public appeal flow, mocking repositories/services so we can assert the state transitions, error handling, and role-based guards around `createAppeal`/`processAppeal` (`backend/src/test/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/AppealServiceTest.java`).

Tests: attempted `cd backend && mvn test -pl . -Dtest=AppealServiceTest -q`, but `mvn` is unavailable in the environment and `./mvnw` is also missing; please rerun (or install Maven) once the toolchain is provisioned so the suite can execute.

I'll summarize the addition of unit tests for AppealService and BackupService, note the attempted test runs that failed due to missing Maven, and include suggestions for the user to install Maven and rerun tests as next steps.

**Summarizing build and security updates**

I'm preparing a final message that highlights the addition of DOMPurify for sanitization with relevant file references, the new security filter implementation, and the successful installation of DOMPurify via npm. Iâ€™ll explain the build failure caused by top-level await unsupported in the current target environment and suggest updating it before building again. Delete the file `schema-reference.sql` from the project root. It is a duplicate of `init-db/01-schema.sql` and adds confusion.

```bash
rm -f schema-reference.sql
```

## Step 2 â€” Add logging to GlobalExceptionHandler

Open `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java`.

Add the `@Slf4j` annotation to the class. Find:

```java
@RestControllerAdvice
public class GlobalExceptionHandler {
```

Replace with:

```java
@Slf4j
@RestControllerAdvice
public class GlobalExceptionHandler {
```

Add the import at the top of the file:

```java
import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
```

Then find the `handleGenericException` method body. Find:

```java
    public ResponseEntity<ApiError> handleGenericException(Exception ex, HttpServletRequest request) {

```

Replace with:

```

## Step 3 â€” Document COOKIE_SECURE and AES_SECRET_KEY in README.md

Open `README.md` and add a "Security Notes" section before the final section. Add this block:

```markdown
## Security Notes

| Variable | Purpose | Default | Production Guidance |
|----------|---------|---------|---------------------|
| `AES_SECRET_KEY` | Column-level AES-256-GCM encryption key | `ThisIsA32ByteKeyForAES256Enc!!` (dev only) | Replace with a securely generated 32-byte key via Docker secrets or `.env` excluded from version control |
| `COOKIE_SECURE` | Sets `Secure` flag on session cookies | Not set (cookies sent over HTTP) | Set to `true` in any HTTPS deployment to prevent session token leakage over unencrypted connections |

> **Important**: The default `AES_SECRET_KEY` in `docker-compose.yml` is for development/demo only. Never use it in production.
```

3: import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
4: import jakarta.validation.ConstraintViolationException;
5: import lombok.Builder;
6: import lombok.Value;
7: import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;
8: import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
9: import org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException;
10: import org.springframework.web.bind.MethodArgumentNotValidException;
11: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ExceptionHandler;
12: import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestControllerAdvice;
13: 
14: import java.time.Instant;
15: import java.util.List;
16: import java.util.stream.Collectors;
17: 
18: @RestControllerAdvice
19: public class GlobalExceptionHandler {
20: 
21:     @ExceptionHandler(MethodArgumentNotValidException.class)
22:     public ResponseEntity<ApiError> handleValidationException(MethodArgumentNotValidException ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
23:         List<FieldErrorDetail> fieldErrors = ex.getBindingResult().getFieldErrors().stream()
24:             .map(error -> new FieldErrorDetail(error.getField(), error.getDefaultMessage()))
25:             .collect(Collectors.toList());
26:         ApiError apiError = ApiError.builder()
27:             .code(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST.value())
28:             .message("Validation failed")
29:             .path(request.getRequestURI())
30:             .timestamp(Instant.now().toString())
31:             .fieldErrors(fieldErrors)
32:             .build();
33:         return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST).body(apiError);
34:     }
35: 
36:     @ExceptionHandler(ConstraintViolationException.class)
37:     public ResponseEntity<ApiError> handleConstraintViolation(ConstraintViolationException ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
38:         List<FieldErrorDetail> fieldErrors = ex.getConstraintViolations().stream()
39:             .map(violation -> new FieldErrorDetail(violation.getPropertyPath().toString(), violation.getMessage()))
40:             .collect(Collectors.toList());
41:         ApiError apiError = ApiError.builder()
42:             .code(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST.value())
43:             .message("Constraint violation")
44:             .path(request.getRequestURI())
45:             .timestamp(Instant.now().toString())
46:             .fieldErrors(fieldErrors)
47:             .build();
48:         return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST).body(apiError);
49:     }
50: 
51:     @ExceptionHandler(BusinessException.class)
52:     public ResponseEntity<ApiError> handleBusinessException(BusinessException ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
53:         HttpStatus status = ex.getStatus();
54:         ApiError apiError = ApiError.builder()
55:             .code(status.value())
56:             .message(ex.getMessage())
57:             .path(request.getRequestURI())
58:             .timestamp(Instant.now().toString())
59:             .fieldErrors(List.of())
60:             .build();
61:         return ResponseEntity.status(status).body(apiError);
62:     }
63: 
64:     @ExceptionHandler(AccessDeniedException.class)
65:     public ResponseEntity<ApiError> handleAccessDenied(AccessDeniedException ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
66:         ApiError apiError = ApiError.builder()
67:             .code(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN.value())
68:             .message("Access denied")
69:             .path(request.getRequestURI())
70:             .timestamp(Instant.now().toString())
71:             .fieldErrors(List.of())
72:             .build();
73:         return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(apiError);
74:     }
75: 
76:     @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class)
77:     public ResponseEntity<ApiError> handleGenericException(Exception ex, HttpServletRequest request) {
78:         ApiError apiError = ApiError.builder()
79:             .code(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.value())
80:             .message("Unexpected server error")
81:             .path(request.getRequestURI())
82:             .timestamp(Instant.now().toString())
83:             .fieldErrors(List.of())
84:             .build();
85:         return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR).body(apiError);
86:     }
87:

90:         String field;
91:         String message;
92:     }
93:

98:         String message;
99:         String timestamp;

102:     }
103: }

files.before:
# ShiftWorks JobOps Platform

A full-stack offline-capable platform for managing part-time job postings with employer, reviewer, and administrator roles. Built with Spring Boot, Vue.js, and MySQL.

## Tech Stack

| Layer    | Technology                                   |
|----------|----------------------------------------------|
| Backend  | Java 17, Spring Boot 3, Spring Security, JPA |
| Frontend | Vue 3, Element Plus, Vite, vue-echarts        |
| Database | MySQL 8                                       |
| Runtime  | Docker, Docker Compose                        |

## Prerequisites

- Docker 20.10+
- Docker Compose v2+

## Quick Start
```
docker compose up --build
```
Wait 30-60 seconds for all services to start.

## Access URLs

| Service  | URL                    |
|----------|------------------------|
| Frontend | http://localhost       |
| Backend  | http://localhost:8080  |
| MySQL    | localhost:3307         |

## Default Credentials

| Username | Password        | Role  |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| admin    | Admin@123456789 | ADMIN |

## User Roles

| Role     | Capabilities |
|----------|-------------|
| EMPLOYER | Create/edit/submit job postings, file appeals, create claims |
| REVIEWER | Approve/reject/takedown postings, process appeals |
| ADMIN    | User management, dictionaries, audit logs, backups, alerts |

## Running Tests
- All tests: `./run_tests.sh`
- Unit tests only: `cd backend && mvn test`
- Individual API test: `./API_tests/test_auth.sh`

## Verification Steps
1. Login as admin (`admin` / `Admin@123456789`)
2. Create employer and reviewer users via Admin â†’ Users
3. Login as employer, create a job posting
4. Submit the posting for review
5. Login as reviewer, approve the posting
6. Login as employer, publish (step-up password required)
7. Check Admin Dashboard for updated metrics and charts
8. Review Admin â†’ Audit Log for recorded actions
9. Trigger a backup from Admin â†’ Backups

## Project Structure
```
â”śâ”€â”€ backend/          # Spring Boot API
â”śâ”€â”€ frontend/         # Vue 3 + Element Plus
â”śâ”€â”€ init-db/          # MySQL schema + seed data
â”śâ”€â”€ API_tests/        # curl-based API test scripts
â”śâ”€â”€ unit_tests/       # JUnit test file copies
â”śâ”€â”€ docs/             # Design doc + API spec
â”śâ”€â”€ docker-compose.yml
â”śâ”€â”€ run_tests.sh
â””â”€â”€ README.md
```

## Key Features
- **Job Lifecycle**: Full status machine (Draft â†’ Review â†’ Approve â†’ Publish)
- **RBAC**: Role-based access on every endpoint (Employer/Reviewer/Admin)
- **Security**: Session-based auth, CAPTCHA, account lock, rate limiting, CSRF
- **AES-256 Encryption**: Column-level encryption for sensitive fields
- **Audit Trails**: Immutable logs with who/what/when/before/after
- **File Handling**: Upload validation, magic bytes, quarantine, PDF watermarks
- **Analytics**: Built-in dashboards, custom builder, scheduled reports, anomaly detection
- **Backup & Restore**: Encrypted nightly backups with admin restore workflow

# ShiftWorks JobOps Platform

A full-stack offline-capable platform for managing part-time job postings with employer, reviewer, and administrator roles. Built with Spring Boot, Vue.js, and MySQL.

## Tech Stack

| Layer    | Technology                                   |
|----------|----------------------------------------------|
| Backend  | Java 17, Spring Boot 3, Spring Security, JPA |
| Frontend | Vue 3, Element Plus, Vite, vue-echarts        |
| Database | MySQL 8                                       |
| Runtime  | Docker, Docker Compose                        |

## Prerequisites

- Docker 20.10+
- Docker Compose v2+

## Quick Start
```
docker compose up --build
```
Wait 30-60 seconds for all services to start.

## Access URLs

| Service  | URL                    |
|----------|------------------------|
| Frontend | http://localhost       |
| Backend  | http://localhost:8080  |
| MySQL    | localhost:3307         |

## Default Credentials

| Username | Password        | Role  |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| admin    | Admin@123456789 | ADMIN |

## User Roles

| Role     | Capabilities |
|----------|-------------|
| EMPLOYER | Create/edit/submit job postings, file appeals, create claims |
| REVIEWER | Approve/reject/takedown postings, process appeals |
| ADMIN    | User management, dictionaries, audit logs, backups, alerts |

## Running Tests
- All tests: `./run_tests.sh`
- Unit tests only: `cd backend && mvn test`
- Individual API test: `./API_tests/test_auth.sh`

## Verification Steps
1. Login as admin (`admin` / `Admin@123456789`)
2. Create employer and reviewer users via Admin â†’ Users
3. Login as employer, create a job posting
4. Submit the posting for review
5. Login as reviewer, approve the posting
6. Login as employer, publish (step-up password required)
7. Check Admin Dashboard for updated metrics and charts
8. Review Admin â†’ Audit Log for recorded actions
9. Trigger a backup from Admin â†’ Backups

## Project Structure
```
â”śâ”€â”€ backend/          # Spring Boot API
â”śâ”€â”€ frontend/         # Vue 3 + Element Plus
â”śâ”€â”€ init-db/          # MySQL schema + seed data
â”śâ”€â”€ API_tests/        # curl-based API test scripts
â”śâ”€â”€ unit_tests/       # JUnit test file copies
â”śâ”€â”€ docs/             # Design doc + API spec
â”śâ”€â”€ docker-compose.yml
â”śâ”€â”€ run_tests.sh
â””â”€â”€ README.md
```

## Key Features
- **Job Lifecycle**: Full status machine (Draft â†’ Review â†’ Approve â†’ Publish)
- **RBAC**: Role-based access on every endpoint (Employer/Reviewer/Admin)
- **Security**: Session-based auth, CAPTCHA, account lock, rate limiting, CSRF
- **AES-256 Encryption**: Column-level encryption for sensitive fields
- **Audit Trails**: Immutable logs with who/what/when/before/after
- **File Handling**: Upload validation, magic bytes, quarantine, PDF watermarks
- **Analytics**: Built-in dashboards, custom builder, scheduled reports, anomaly detection
- **Backup & Restore**: Encrypted nightly backups with admin restore workflow

A full-stack offline-capable platform for managing part-time job postings with employer, reviewer, and administrator roles. Built with Spring Boot, Vue.js, and MySQL.

## Prerequisites

- Docker 20.10+
- Docker Compose v2+

## Quick Start
```
docker compose up --build
```
Wait 30-60 seconds for all services to start.

## Access URLs

| Service  | URL                    |
|----------|------------------------|
| Frontend | http://localhost       |
| Backend  | http://localhost:8080  |
| MySQL    | localhost:3307         |

## Default Credentials

| Username | Password        | Role  |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| admin    | Admin@123456789 | ADMIN |

## User Roles

| Role     | Capabilities |
|----------|-------------|
| EMPLOYER | Create/edit/submit job postings, file appeals, create claims |
| REVIEWER | Approve/reject/takedown postings, process appeals |
| ADMIN    | User management, dictionaries, audit logs, backups, alerts |

- All tests: `./run_tests.sh`
- Unit tests only: `cd backend && mvn test`
- Individual API test: `./API_tests/test_auth.sh`

1. Login as admin (`admin` / `Admin@123456789`)
2. Create employer and reviewer users via Admin â†’ Users

4. Submit the posting for review
5. Login as reviewer, approve the posting
6. Login as employer, publish (step-up password required)
7. Check Admin Dashboard for updated metrics and charts
8. Review Admin â†’ Audit Log for recorded actions
9. Trigger a backup from Admin â†’ Backups

## Project Structure
```
â”śâ”€â”€ backend/          # Spring Boot API
â”śâ”€â”€ frontend/         # Vue 3 + Element Plus

- **Job Lifecycle**: Full status machine (Draft â†’ Review â†’ Approve â†’ Publish)

- **Security**: Session-based auth, CAPTCHA, account lock, rate limiting, CSRF
- **AES-256 Encryption**: Column-level encryption for sensitive fields
- **Audit Trails**: Immutable logs with who/what/when/before/after
- **File Handling**: Upload validation, magic bytes, quarantine, PDF watermarks
- **Analytics**: Built-in dashboards, custom builder, scheduled reports, anomaly detection
- **Backup & Restore**: Encrypted nightly backups with admin restore workflow

| EMPLOYER | Create/edit/submit job postings, file appeals, create claims |
| REVIEWER | Approve/reject/takedown postings, process appeals |
| ADMIN    | User management, dictionaries, audit logs, backups, alerts |

## Running Tests
- All tests: `./run_tests.sh`
- Unit tests only: `cd backend && mvn test`
- Individual API test: `./API_tests/test_auth.sh`

## Verification Steps
1. Login as admin (`admin` / `Admin@123456789`)
2. Create employer and reviewer users via Admin â†’ Users
3. Login as employer, create a job posting
4. Submit the posting for review
5. Login as reviewer, approve the posting
6. Login as employer, publish (step-up password required)
7. Check Admin Dashboard for updated metrics and charts

122:         AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();
123:         if (!stepUpVerificationService.verify(user.id(), request.stepUpPassword())) {
124:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "stepUpPassword: Verification failed");

128:         return Map.of("contactPhone", job.getContactPhone() != null ? job.getContactPhone() : "");

content.text:
- Added three new `@WebMvcTest` integration suites that import the real security filter chain to validate public auth routes, admin RBAC, and job lifecycle endpoints without a backing database (`AuthIntegrationTest`, `RbacIntegrationTest`, `JobLifecycleIntegrationTest` under `backend/src/test/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/integration`). Each test mocks the required service beans so Spring can wire the controllers + filters while exercising authentication rules and security headers.

**Tests**
- `cd backend && mvn test -pl . -Dtest="AuthIntegrationTest,RbacIntegrationTest" -q` *(fails here because `mvn` is not installed in this environment; please rerun once Maven or the wrapper is available).*

content.text:
- Replaced the top-level `await` in `frontend/src/main.js` with a Promise-based bootstrapping sequence so `authStore.restoreSession()` runs before the router is mounted, fixing the Vite/esbuild build failure and keeping the app initialization flow intact.
- `npm run build` now succeeds (no more `Top-level await` error).

Tests:
- `npm run build` (frontend) âś…

# Fix: Remove Redundant unit_tests/ Directory

## Priority: LOW â€” Engineering quality cleanup

## Directory to delete
- `unit_tests/` (at the project root)

## Problem

The `unit_tests/` directory contains 6 Java test files that are exact duplicates of the files already present in `backend/src/test/java/`. They are not on the Maven test classpath and are never executed by `mvn test`. They add confusion about which test files are authoritative.

## Instructions

Delete the entire `unit_tests/` directory:

```bash
rm -rf unit_tests/
```

## Verification

Run: `ls unit_tests/` â€” should return "No such file or directory"

The authoritative test files remain at `backend/src/test/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/`.

- Removed the obsolete `unit_tests/` directory so only the Maven-managed `backend/src/test/java` tests remain (`unit_tests/` no longer exists).
- `ls unit_tests` now reports â€śNo such file or directory,â€ť confirming the cleanup.

# Fix: Add Approval Rate & Avg Handling Time Widgets to Admin Dashboard

## Priority: MEDIUM â€” Two of four explicitly required admin metrics are invisible

## Files to modify
- `frontend/src/views/AdminDashboardView.vue`

## Problem

The admin dashboard fetches `approvalRate` and `avgHandling` data from the API (lines 33-34) and computes `approvalRatePercent` and `avgHandlingHours` (lines 70-71), but the template never renders them. The prompt requires dashboards showing "post volume, claim success rate, average handling time, and reviewer activity." Two of these four are dead code.

## Instructions

Open `frontend/src/views/AdminDashboardView.vue`.

Find this block in the `metricCards` computed property (around line 73):

```javascript
const metricCards = computed(() => ([
  { label: 'Total Users', value: stats.value.totalUsers },
  { label: 'Active Postings', value: stats.value.activePostings },
  { label: 'Pending Reviews', value: stats.value.pendingReviews },
  { label: 'Open Claims', value: stats.value.openClaims },
  { label: 'Open Tickets', value: stats.value.openTickets },
  { label: 'Unread Alerts', value: stats.value.unreadAlerts }
]));
```

Replace with:

```javascript
const metricCards = computed(() => ([
  { label: 'Total Users', value: stats.value.totalUsers },
  { label: 'Active Postings', value: stats.value.activePostings },
  { label: 'Pending Reviews', value: stats.value.pendingReviews },
  { label: 'Open Claims', value: stats.value.openClaims },
  { label: 'Approval Rate', value: approvalRatePercent.value + '%' },
  { label: 'Avg Handling Time', value: avgHandlingHours.value + 'h' }
]));
```

This replaces the less important "Open Tickets" and "Unread Alerts" cards with the two explicitly required metrics from the prompt. If you want all 8, you can keep all 6 originals and add 2 more â€” but the important thing is that approval rate and handling time are now visible.

## Verification

After saving, confirm:
1. The `metricCards` computed includes entries for 'Approval Rate' and 'Avg Handling Time'
2. `npm run build` in `frontend/` completes without errors

content.text:
**Admin Metrics**

- Added â€śApproval Rateâ€ť and â€śAvg Handling Timeâ€ť cards to the admin dashboard so the previously-fetched stats are now displayed alongside users/active postings/pending reviews/open claims (`frontend/src/views/AdminDashboardView.vue`).
- Frontend build: `npm run build` (frontend) âś…

# Fix: Dashboard Preview Auth Guard + SecurityConfig Deny-All

## Priority: MEDIUM â€” Preview endpoint has no role check; SecurityConfig too permissive

## Files to modify
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/controller/DashboardController.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/config/SecurityConfig.java`

## Problem 1 â€” Dashboard preview has no authorization guard

`DashboardController.preview()` at line 66 runs analytics queries with user-supplied parameters but has no `@PreAuthorize` and doesn't even accept an `Authentication` parameter. Any authenticated user (including employers) can run analytics queries.

## Problem 2 â€” `anyRequest().permitAll()` is overly permissive

`SecurityConfig` sets `.anyRequest().permitAll()` after restricting `/api/**`. If actuator endpoints like `/actuator/env` or `/actuator/beans` are enabled in the future, they'd be completely open.

## Step 1 â€” Add auth guard to dashboard preview

Open `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/controller/DashboardController.java`.

Add the import:

```java
import org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;
```

Find the preview method (around line 66):

```java
    @PostMapping("/preview")
    public List<Map<String, Object>> preview(@Valid @RequestBody DashboardPreviewRequest request) {
        return service.preview(request);
    }
```

Replace with:

```java
    @PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('ADMIN', 'REVIEWER')")
    @PostMapping("/preview")
    public List<Map<String, Object>> preview(@Valid @RequestBody DashboardPreviewRequest request) {
        return service.preview(request);
    }
```

## Step 2 â€” Change anyRequest to denyAll

Open `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/config/SecurityConfig.java`.

Find this line:

```java
                .anyRequest().permitAll());
```

Replace with:

```java
                .anyRequest().denyAll());
```

## Verification

After saving, confirm:
1. The `preview` method has `@PreAuthorize("hasAnyRole('ADMIN', 'REVIEWER')")`
2. `SecurityConfig` uses `.anyRequest().denyAll()`
3. The project compiles: `cd backend && mvn compile -q`

- Hardened the security configuration by changing `.anyRequest().permitAll()` to `.anyRequest().denyAll()`, ensuring future actuator or other endpoints stay protected unless explicitly opened (`backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/config/SecurityConfig.java`).

content.text:
- Added `payBounds` computed metadata so the hourly form enforces the $12â€“$75 window (flat pay still capped at $5,000) and wired it into the `el-input-number` min/max binding, ensuring the UI reflects backend limits (`frontend/src/views/JobPostingForm.vue`).
- Replaced the pay amount rule with a custom validator that reports the current bounds message and triggers re-validation when the pay type changes, giving immediate inline feedback instead of only after submitting (`frontend/src/views/JobPostingForm.vue`).

**Tests**
- `npm run build` (frontend) âś…

# Fix: Add Structured Logging to Services

## Priority: MEDIUM â€” Most services produce zero log output

## Files to modify (add `@Slf4j` and log statements)
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/AuthService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/JobPostingService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/ReviewService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/AppealService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/ClaimService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/TicketService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/AdminUserService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/SessionService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/DashboardService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/StepUpVerificationService.java`

## Problem

Only 5 of 24 service files have loggers. Critical operations like login, job creation, review decisions, and user management produce no log output. Production troubleshooting would be extremely difficult.

## Instructions

For each file listed above, do the following:

### 1. Add `@Slf4j` annotation

If the class does NOT already have `@Slf4j`, add it above the class declaration. Add the import `import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;` at the top.

### 2. Add log statements at key operations

Use these patterns â€” add `log.info(...)` at the start of each key public method:

**AuthService.java:**
- In `login()`: `log.info("Login attempt for user={}", request.username());`
- In `register()`: `log.info("Registration attempt for user={}", request.username());`
- In `changePassword()`: `log.info("Password change for userId={}", user.id());`

**JobPostingService.java:**
- In the create method: `log.info("Creating job draft for employerId={}", user.id());`
- In the submit method: `log.info("Submitting job id={} for review", jobId);`
- In the publish method: `log.info("Publishing job id={}", jobId);`

**ReviewService.java:**
- In `approve()`: `log.info("Approving job id={} by reviewer={}", jobId, reviewer.username());`
- In `reject()`: `log.info("Rejecting job id={} by reviewer={}", jobId, reviewer.username());`
- In `takedown()`: `log.info("Takedown of job id={} by reviewer={}", jobId, reviewer.username());`

**AppealService.java:**
- In `createAppeal()`: `log.info("Appeal created for jobId={} by employer={}", request.jobPostingId(), employer.username());`
- In `processAppeal()`: `log.info("Processing appeal id={} decision={} by reviewer={}", id, request.decision(), reviewer.username());`

**ClaimService.java:**
- In create method: `log.info("Claim created for jobId={} by userId={}", request.jobPostingId(), user.id());`

**TicketService.java:**
- In create method: `log.info("Ticket created type={} by userId={}", request.type(), user.id());`

**AdminUserService.java:**
- In create user method: `log.info("Admin creating user={} role={}", request.username(), request.role());`
- In role change method: `log.info("Admin changing role for userId={} to {}", userId, newRole);`

**SessionService.java:**
- In `createSession()`: `log.info("Session created for userId={}", user.getId());`
- In `invalidateSession()`: `log.info("Session invalidated id={}", session.getId());`
- In `invalidateAllSessions()`: `log.info("All sessions invalidated for userId={}", userId);`

**DashboardService.java:**
- In `create()`: `log.info("Dashboard created name={} by userId={}", request.name(), user.id());`
- In `export()`: `log.info("Dashboard export id={} masked={} by userId={}", id, masked, user.id());`

**StepUpVerificationService.java:**
- In `verify()`: `log.info("Step-up verification for userId={} result={}", userId, result);` (where `result` is the boolean return)

## Verification

After saving, confirm:
1. Each modified file has `@Slf4j` and at least one `log.info(...)` call
2. The project compiles: `cd backend && mvn compile -q`

29: public class AuthService {
30: 
31:     private final UserRepository userRepository;
32:     private final PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder;
33:     private final PasswordPolicyService passwordPolicyService;
34:     private final SessionService sessionService;
35:     private final AuditService auditService;
36:     private final AppProperties appProperties;
37: 
38:     public static final String LOGIN_FAILURE = "Invalid username or password";
39: 
40:     @Transactional
41:     public LoginResult login(LoginRequest request, HttpServletRequest httpRequest, boolean captchaPassed) {
42:         Optional<User> userOptional = userRepository.findByUsername(request.username());
43:         if (userOptional.isEmpty()) {
44:             int remaining = appProperties.getSecurity().getLockAfterFailures();
45:             return LoginResult.failure(false, remaining);
46:         }
47:         User user = userOptional.get();
48:         validateLockState(user);
49:         boolean captchaRequired = user.getFailedLoginAttempts() >= appProperties.getSecurity().getCaptchaAfterFailures();
50:         if (captchaRequired && !captchaPassed) {
51:             int remaining = appProperties.getSecurity().getLockAfterFailures() - user.getFailedLoginAttempts();
52:             return LoginResult.failure(true, Math.max(0, remaining));
53:         }
54:         if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.password(), user.getPasswordHash())) {
55:             auditService.log(user.getId(), "USER_LOGIN_FAILED", "USER", user.getId(), null, null);
56:             int remaining = registerFailure(user);
57:             boolean requireCaptcha = user.getFailedLoginAttempts() >= appProperties.getSecurity().getCaptchaAfterFailures();
58:             return LoginResult.failure(requireCaptcha, Math.max(0, remaining));
59:         }
60:         resetFailures(user);
61:         auditService.log(user.getId(), "USER_LOGIN", "USER", user.getId(), null, null);
62:         boolean passwordExpired = user.getPasswordChangedAt() != null && user.getPasswordChangedAt()
63:             .isBefore(Instant.now().minus(appProperties.getSecurity().getPassword().getRotationDays(), ChronoUnit.DAYS));
64:         UserSession session = sessionService.createSession(user, httpRequest.getRemoteAddr(), httpRequest.getHeader("User-Agent"));
65:         return LoginResult.success(new LoginResponse(UserResponse.from(user), session.getCsrfToken(), passwordExpired), session.getId());
66:     }
67: 
68:     private void validateLockState(User user) {
69:         if (user.getStatus() == UserStatus.DISABLED) {
70:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "Account disabled");
71:         }
72:         if (user.getStatus() == UserStatus.LOCKED) {
73:             if (user.getLockedUntil() != null && user.getLockedUntil().isAfter(Instant.now())) {
74:                 throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.LOCKED, "Account locked until " + user.getLockedUntil());
75:             }
76:             user.setStatus(UserStatus.ACTIVE);
77:             user.setLockedUntil(null);
78:             user.setFailedLoginAttempts(0);
79:             userRepository.save(user);
80:         }
81:     }
82: 
83:     private int registerFailure(User user) {
84:         int attempts = user.getFailedLoginAttempts() + 1;
85:         user.setFailedLoginAttempts(attempts);
86:         boolean captchaToggle = attempts >= appProperties.getSecurity().getCaptchaAfterFailures();
87:         user.setCaptchaRequired(captchaToggle);
88:         if (attempts >= appProperties.getSecurity().getLockAfterFailures()) {
89:             user.setStatus(UserStatus.LOCKED);
90:             user.setLockedUntil(Instant.now().plus(appProperties.getSecurity().getLockDurationMinutes(), ChronoUnit.MINUTES));
91:         }
92:         userRepository.save(user);
93:         return appProperties.getSecurity().getLockAfterFailures() - attempts;
94:     }
95: 
96:     private void resetFailures(User user) {
97:         user.setFailedLoginAttempts(0);
98:         user.setCaptchaRequired(false);
99:         user.setStatus(UserStatus.ACTIVE);
100:         user.setLockedUntil(null);
101:         userRepository.save(user);
102:     }
103: 
104:     @Transactional
105:     public UserResponse register(RegisterRequest request) {
106:         if (userRepository.findByUsername(request.username()).isPresent()) {
107:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, "Username already taken");
108:         }
109:         if (userRepository.findByEmail(request.email()).isPresent()) {
110:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, "Email already registered");
111:         }
112:         var violations = passwordPolicyService.validate(request.password());
113:         if (!violations.isEmpty()) {
114:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, String.join("; ", violations));
115:         }
116:         User user = new User();
117:         user.setUsername(request.username());
118:         user.setEmail(request.email());
119:         user.setRole(UserRole.EMPLOYER);
120:         user.setStatus(UserStatus.ACTIVE);
121:         user.setPasswordHash(passwordEncoder.encode(request.password()));
122:         user.setPasswordChangedAt(Instant.now());
123:         user.setCreatedAt(Instant.now());
124:         user.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
125:         user.setCaptchaRequired(false);
126:         User saved = userRepository.save(user);
127:         auditService.log(saved.getId(), "USER_CREATED", "USER", saved.getId(), null, saved);
128:         return UserResponse.from(saved);
129:     }
130: 
131:     @Transactional
132:     public void logout(Long userId) {
133:         auditService.log(userId, "USER_LOGOUT", "USER", userId, null, null);
134:         sessionService.invalidateAllSessions(userId);
135:     }
136: 
137:     @Transactional
138:     public void changePassword(Long userId, ChangePasswordRequest request) {
139:         User user = userRepository.findById(userId)
140:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "User not found"));
141:         if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.currentPassword(), user.getPasswordHash())) {
142:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "Current password is incorrect");
143:         }
144:         var violations = passwordPolicyService.validate(request.newPassword());
145:         if (!violations.isEmpty()) {
146:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, String.join("; ", violations));
147:         }
148:         user.setPasswordHash(passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword()));
149:         user.setPasswordChangedAt(Instant.now());
150:         userRepository.save(user);
151:         auditService.log(userId, "PASSWORD_CHANGED", "USER", userId, null, null);
152:         sessionService.invalidateAllSessions(userId);
153:     }
154: 
155:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
156:     public UserResponse me(Long userId) {
157:         User user = userRepository.findById(userId)
158:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "User not found"));
159:         return UserResponse.from(user);
160:     }
161: 
162:     public record LoginResult(LoginResponse response, LoginFailure failure, String sessionToken) {
163:         public static LoginResult success(LoginResponse response, String sessionToken) {
164:             return new LoginResult(response, null, sessionToken);
165:         }
166: 
167:         public static LoginResult failure(boolean captchaRequired, int remainingAttempts) {
168:             return new LoginResult(null, new LoginFailure(captchaRequired, remainingAttempts), null);
169:         }
170: 
171:         public boolean isSuccess() {
172:             return response != null;
173:         }
174:     }
175: 
176:     public record LoginFailure(boolean captchaRequired, int remainingAttempts) {
177:     }
178: }

189:         long pending = jobPostingRepository.countByEmployer_IdAndStatus(user.id(), JobStatus.PENDING_REVIEW);

197:     private Specification<JobPosting> buildSpecification(AuthenticatedUser user, String statusValue, Long categoryId, Long locationId, String search) {
198:         return (root, query, cb) -> {
199:             query.distinct(true);
200:             List<Predicate> predicates = new ArrayList<>();
201:             if (user.role() == UserRole.EMPLOYER) {
202:                 predicates.add(cb.equal(root.get("employer").get("id"), user.id()));
203:             } else if (user.role() == UserRole.REVIEWER) {
204:                 predicates.add(root.get("status").in(List.of(JobStatus.PENDING_REVIEW, JobStatus.PUBLISHED)));
205:             }
206:             if (statusValue != null && !statusValue.isBlank()) {
207:                 JobStatus status = parseStatus(statusValue);
208:                 predicates.add(cb.equal(root.get("status"), status));
209:             }
210:             if (categoryId != null) {
211:                 predicates.add(cb.equal(root.get("category").get("id"), categoryId));
212:             }
213:             if (locationId != null) {
214:                 predicates.add(cb.equal(root.get("location").get("id"), locationId));
215:             }
216:             if (search != null && !search.isBlank()) {
217:                 String like = "%" + search.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + "%";
218:                 predicates.add(cb.or(
219:                     cb.like(cb.lower(root.get("title")), like),
220:                     cb.like(cb.lower(root.get("description")), like)
221:                 ));
222:             }
223:             return cb.and(predicates.toArray(new Predicate[0]));
224:         };
225:     }

227:     private Sort buildSort(String sort, String direction) {
228:         String sortField = (sort == null || sort.isBlank()) ? "createdAt" : sort;
229:         if (!List.of("createdAt", "payAmount").contains(sortField)) {
230:             sortField = "createdAt";
231:         }
232:         Sort.Direction dir = "asc".equalsIgnoreCase(direction) ? Sort.Direction.ASC : Sort.Direction.DESC;
233:         return Sort.by(dir, sortField);
234:     }

236:     private void applyRequest(JobPosting jobPosting, JobPostingRequest request, boolean allowDefaultValidity) {
237:         validateBaseRequest(request);
238:         Category category = categoryRepository.findById(request.categoryId())
239:             .filter(Category::isActive)
240:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "categoryId: Category is inactive or missing"));
241:         Location location = locationRepository.findById(request.locationId())
242:             .filter(Location::isActive)
243:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "locationId: Location is inactive or missing"));
244:         jobPosting.setTitle(request.title().trim());
245:         jobPosting.setDescription(request.description().trim());
246:         jobPosting.setCategory(category);
247:         jobPosting.setLocation(location);
248:         jobPosting.setPayType(request.payType());
249:         jobPosting.setSettlementType(request.settlementType());
250:         jobPosting.setPayAmount(request.payAmount().setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP));
251:         jobPosting.setHeadcount(request.headcount());
252:         jobPosting.setWeeklyHours(request.weeklyHours().setScale(1, RoundingMode.HALF_UP));
253:         jobPosting.setContactPhone(request.contactPhone());
254:         LocalDate startDate = request.validityStart() != null ? request.validityStart() : LocalDate.now();
255:         LocalDate defaultEnd = startDate.plusDays(appProperties.getJobValidation().getValidityDays().getMin());
256:         LocalDate endDate = request.validityEnd() != null ? request.validityEnd() : defaultEnd;
257:         if (!allowDefaultValidity && request.validityEnd() == null) {
258:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "validityEnd: Validity end date is required");
259:         }
260:         validateDates(startDate, endDate, false);
261:         jobPosting.setValidityStart(startDate);
262:         jobPosting.setValidityEnd(endDate);
263:         applyTags(jobPosting, request.tags());
264:     }
265: 
266:     private void applyTags(JobPosting jobPosting, List<String> tags) {
267:         jobPosting.getTags().clear();
268:         if (tags == null) {
269:             return;
270:         }
271:         if (tags.size() > 10) {
272:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "tags: Maximum of 10 tags allowed");
273:         }
274:         tags.stream()
275:             .map(tag -> tag == null ? "" : tag.trim())
276:             .filter(tag -> !tag.isEmpty())
277:             .map(tag -> {
278:                 if (tag.length() > 30) {
279:                     throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "tags: Tag '" + tag + "' exceeds 30 characters");
280:                 }
281:                 return tag;
282:             })
283:             .forEach(tag -> {
284:                 JobPostingTag tagEntity = new JobPostingTag();
285:                 tagEntity.setJobPosting(jobPosting);
286:                 tagEntity.setTagName(tag);
287:                 jobPosting.getTags().add(tagEntity);
288:             });
289:     }
290: 
291:     private void validateBaseRequest(JobPostingRequest request) {
292:         if (request.title() == null || request.title().trim().length() < 5 || request.title().trim().length() > 200) {
293:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "title: Title must be between 5 and 200 characters");
294:         }
295:         if (request.description() == null || request.description().trim().length() < 20 || request.description().trim().length() > 5000) {
296:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "description: Description must be between 20 and 5000 characters");
297:         }
298:         var jv = appProperties.getJobValidation();
299:         if (request.payType() == PayType.HOURLY) {
300:             validateRange("payAmount", request.payAmount(),
301:                 BigDecimal.valueOf(jv.getHourlyPay().getMin()),
302:                 BigDecimal.valueOf(jv.getHourlyPay().getMax()));
303:         } else {
304:             validateRange("payAmount", request.payAmount(),
305:                 BigDecimal.valueOf(jv.getFlatPay().getMin()),
306:                 BigDecimal.valueOf(jv.getFlatPay().getMax()));
307:         }
308:         validateIntRange("headcount", request.headcount(),
309:             jv.getHeadcount().getMin(), jv.getHeadcount().getMax());
310:         validateBigDecimalRange("weeklyHours", request.weeklyHours(),
311:             BigDecimal.valueOf(jv.getWeeklyHours().getMin()),
312:             BigDecimal.valueOf(jv.getWeeklyHours().getMax()));
313:         if (!PhoneUtils.isValidUsPhone(request.contactPhone())) {
314:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "contactPhone: Must be a valid US phone number");
315:         }
316:     }
317: 
318:     private void validateExistingJob(JobPosting job) {

320:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "title: Title must be between 5 and 200 characters");

49:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
50:     public PageResponse<AppealResponse> listAppeals(AuthenticatedUser user, String statusValue, int page, int size) {
51:         if (user.role() != UserRole.REVIEWER && user.role() != UserRole.EMPLOYER) {
52:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "appeal: Access denied");
53:         }
54:         if (user.role() == UserRole.REVIEWER && (statusValue == null || statusValue.isBlank())) {
55:             // Default reviewers to pending appeals per spec
56:             statusValue = AppealStatus.PENDING.name();
57:         }
58:         Pageable pageable = PageRequest.of(page, size, Sort.by(Sort.Direction.ASC, "createdAt"));
59:         Specification<Appeal> spec = (root, query, cb) -> {
60:             Predicate predicate = cb.conjunction();
61:             if (user.role() == UserRole.EMPLOYER) {
62:                 predicate = cb.and(predicate, cb.equal(root.get("employer").get("id"), user.id()));
63:             }
64:             if (statusValue != null && !statusValue.isBlank()) {
65:                 AppealStatus status = AppealStatus.valueOf(statusValue.toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT));
66:                 predicate = cb.and(predicate, cb.equal(root.get("status"), status));
67:             }
68:             return predicate;
69:         };
70:         Page<Appeal> result = appealRepository.findAll(spec, pageable);
71:         List<AppealResponse> items = result.getContent().stream()
72:             .map(this::toResponse)
73:             .toList();
74:         return new PageResponse<>(items, result.getTotalElements(), page, size, result.getTotalPages());
75:     }

77:     @Transactional
78:     public void createAppeal(AppealCreateRequest request, AuthenticatedUser employer) {
79:         if (employer.role() != UserRole.EMPLOYER) {
80:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "appeal: Employers only");
81:         }
82:         JobPosting jobPosting = jobPostingRepository.findByIdAndEmployer_Id(request.jobPostingId(), employer.id())
83:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "jobPosting: Not found"));
84:         if (jobPosting.getStatus() != JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN) {
85:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "jobPosting: Only taken down postings can be appealed");
86:         }
87:         if (appealRepository.existsByJobPosting_IdAndStatus(jobPosting.getId(), AppealStatus.PENDING)) {
88:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "appeal: Pending appeal already exists");
89:         }
90:         Appeal appeal = new Appeal();
91:         appeal.setJobPosting(jobPosting);
92:         appeal.setEmployer(jobPosting.getEmployer());
93:         appeal.setAppealReason(request.appealReason());
94:         appeal.setStatus(AppealStatus.PENDING);
95:         appealRepository.save(appeal);
96: 
97:         jobPosting.setStatus(JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING);
98:         jobPostingRepository.save(jobPosting);
99:         jobHistoryService.record(jobPosting, JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN, JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING, jobPosting.getEmployer(), request.appealReason());
100:         auditService.log(employer.id(), "APPEAL_CREATED", "APPEAL", appeal.getId(), null, appeal);
101:     }

106:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "appeal: Access denied");
107:         }
108:         Appeal appeal = appealRepository.findById(id)
109:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "appeal: Not found"));
110:         if (user.role() == UserRole.EMPLOYER && !appeal.getEmployer().getId().equals(user.id())) {
111:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "appeal: Access denied");

118:     @Transactional
119:     public void processAppeal(Long id, AppealProcessRequest request, AuthenticatedUser reviewer) {
120:         if (reviewer.role() != UserRole.REVIEWER) {
121:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "appeal: Reviewers only");
122:         }
123:         Appeal appeal = appealRepository.findById(id)

128:         JobPosting jobPosting = appeal.getJobPosting();
129:         User reviewerUser = userRepository.findById(reviewer.id())

131:         String decision = request.decision().toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT);
132:         switch (decision) {

135:                 jobPosting.setStatus(JobStatus.PUBLISHED);
136:                 jobPosting.setPublishedAt(Instant.now());
137:                 jobHistoryService.record(jobPosting, JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING, JobStatus.PUBLISHED, reviewerUser, request.reviewerRationale());
138:             }
139:             case "DENIED" -> {
140:                 appeal.setStatus(AppealStatus.DENIED);
141:                 jobPosting.setStatus(JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN);
142:                 jobHistoryService.record(jobPosting, JobStatus.APPEAL_PENDING, JobStatus.TAKEN_DOWN, reviewerUser, request.reviewerRationale());
143:             }
144:             default -> throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "decision: Must be GRANTED or DENIED");
145:         }
146:         appeal.setReviewerRationale(request.reviewerRationale());
147:         appeal.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
148:         appealRepository.save(appeal);
149:         jobPostingRepository.save(jobPosting);
150:         auditService.log(reviewer.id(), "APPEAL_PROCESSED", "APPEAL", id, null, appeal);
151:     }
152: 
153:     private AppealResponse toResponse(Appeal appeal) {
154:         return new AppealResponse(
155:             appeal.getId(),
156:             appeal.getJobPosting().getId(),
157:             appeal.getJobPosting().getTitle(),
158:             appeal.getEmployer().getUsername(),
159:             appeal.getAppealReason(),
160:             appeal.getStatus(),
161:             appeal.getCreatedAt()
162:         );
163:     }
164: 
165:     private AppealDetailResponse toDetailResponse(Appeal appeal, ReviewAction takedownAction) {
166:         JobPosting job = appeal.getJobPosting();
167:         String location = job.getLocation().getCity() + ", " + job.getLocation().getState();
168:         String paySummary = "$" + job.getPayAmount().setScale(2, RoundingMode.HALF_UP) + (job.getPayType().name().equals("HOURLY") ? "/hour" : " flat");
169:         return new AppealDetailResponse(
170:             appeal.getId(),
171:             appeal.getStatus(),
172:             appeal.getAppealReason(),
173:             appeal.getCreatedAt(),
174:             appeal.getEmployer().getUsername(),
175:             job.getTitle(),
176:             job.getCategory().getName(),
177:             location,
178:             paySummary,
179:             job.getTakedownReason(),
180:             takedownAction != null ? takedownAction.getRationale() : null,
181:             takedownAction != null ? takedownAction.getCreatedAt() : null,
182:             appeal.getReviewerRationale(),
183:             appeal.getUpdatedAt()
184:         );

116:     private void ensureTransition(ClaimStatus current, ClaimStatus next) {

134:                 }
135:             }
136:             default -> throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "status: Cannot transition from " + current);
137:         }

15: import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
16: import org.springframework.data.domain.Page;

24: 
25: import java.security.SecureRandom;
26: import java.time.Instant;
27: import java.util.Locale;
28: 
29: @Service
30: @RequiredArgsConstructor
31: public class AdminUserService {
32: 
33:     private static final String PASSWORD_CHARS = "ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijkmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789!@#$%^&*";
34: 
35:     private final UserRepository userRepository;
36:     private final PasswordPolicyService passwordPolicyService;
37:     private final PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder;
38:     private final StepUpVerificationService stepUpVerificationService;
39:     private final AuditService auditService;
40:     private final SecureRandom secureRandom = new SecureRandom();
41: 
42:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
43:     public PageResponse<AdminUserResponse> listUsers(String role, String status, String search, int page, int size) {
44:         Pageable pageable = PageRequest.of(page, size, Sort.by(Sort.Direction.DESC, "createdAt"));
45:         Page<User> result = userRepository.findAll((root, query, cb) -> {
46:             var predicate = cb.conjunction();
47:             if (role != null && !role.isBlank()) {
48:                 predicate = cb.and(predicate, cb.equal(root.get("role"), UserRole.valueOf(role.toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT))));
49:             }
50:             if (status != null && !status.isBlank()) {
51:                 predicate = cb.and(predicate, cb.equal(root.get("status"), UserStatus.valueOf(status.toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT))));
52:             }
53:             if (search != null && !search.isBlank()) {
54:                 String like = "%" + search.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) + "%";
55:                 predicate = cb.and(predicate, cb.or(
56:                     cb.like(cb.lower(root.get("username")), like),
57:                     cb.like(cb.lower(root.get("email")), like)
58:                 ));
59:             }
60:             return predicate;
61:         }, pageable);
62:         var items = result.getContent().stream().map(this::toResponse).toList();
63:         return new PageResponse<>(items, result.getTotalElements(), page, size, result.getTotalPages());
64:     }
65: 
66:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
67:     public AdminUserResponse getUser(Long id) {
68:         return userRepository.findById(id)
69:             .map(this::toResponse)
70:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "user: Not found"));
71:     }
72: 
73:     @Transactional
74:     public AdminUserResponse create(AdminUserCreateRequest request) {
75:         if (userRepository.findByUsername(request.username()).isPresent()) {
76:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, "username: Already exists");
77:         }
78:         if (userRepository.findByEmail(request.email()).isPresent()) {
79:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, "email: Already exists");
80:         }
81:         var violations = passwordPolicyService.validate(request.password());
82:         if (!violations.isEmpty()) {
83:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, violations.get(0));
84:         }
85:         User user = new User();
86:         user.setUsername(request.username());
87:         user.setEmail(request.email());
88:         user.setRole(request.role());
89:         user.setStatus(UserStatus.ACTIVE);
90:         user.setPasswordHash(passwordEncoder.encode(request.password()));
91:         user.setPasswordChangedAt(Instant.now());
92:         user.setCreatedAt(Instant.now());
93:         user.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
94:         userRepository.save(user);
95:         auditService.log(null, "USER_CREATED", "USER", user.getId(), null, user);
96:         return toResponse(user);
97:     }
98: 
99:     @Transactional
100:     public AdminUserResponse update(Long id, AdminUserUpdateRequest request, AuthenticatedUser actor) {
101:         User user = userRepository.findById(id)
102:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "user: Not found"));
103:         if (request.email() != null) {
104:             userRepository.findByEmail(request.email()).ifPresent(existing -> {
105:                 if (!existing.getId().equals(user.getId())) {
106:                     throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, "email: Email already in use");
107:                 }
108:             });
109:             user.setEmail(request.email());
110:         }
111:         if (request.status() != null) {
112:             if (actor.id().equals(user.getId()) && request.status() == UserStatus.DISABLED) {
113:                 throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "status: Cannot disable your own account");
114:             }
115:             UserStatus oldStatus = user.getStatus();
116:             user.setStatus(request.status());
117:             if (oldStatus != request.status()) {
118:                 auditService.log(actor.id(), "USER_STATUS_CHANGED", "USER", id, oldStatus, request.status());
119:             }
120:         }
121:         user.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
122:         userRepository.save(user);
123:         return toResponse(user);
124:     }
125: 
126:     @Transactional
127:     public void changeRole(Long id, AdminUserRoleChangeRequest request, AuthenticatedUser actor) {
128:         if (actor.id().equals(id)) {
129:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "role: Cannot change your own role");
130:         }
131:         if (!stepUpVerificationService.verify(actor.id(), request.stepUpPassword())) {
132:             throw new BusinessException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, "stepUpPassword: Verification failed");
133:         }
134:         User user = userRepository.findById(id)
135:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "user: Not found"));
136:         UserRole oldRole = user.getRole();
137:         user.setRole(request.role());
138:         user.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
139:         userRepository.save(user);
140:         auditService.log(actor.id(), "USER_ROLE_CHANGED", "USER", id, oldRole, request.role());
141:     }

145:         User user = userRepository.findById(id)
146:             .orElseThrow(() -> new BusinessException(HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, "user: Not found"));
147:         if (user.getStatus() != UserStatus.LOCKED) {

149:         }
150:         user.setStatus(UserStatus.ACTIVE);
151:         user.setFailedLoginAttempts(0);
152:         user.setLockedUntil(null);
153:         user.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
154:         userRepository.save(user);
155:     }

159:         User user = userRepository.findById(id)

161:         String password = generatePassword();
162:         user.setPasswordHash(passwordEncoder.encode(password));
163:         user.setPasswordChangedAt(Instant.ofEpochSecond(0));
164:         user.setUpdatedAt(Instant.now());
165:         userRepository.save(user);
166:         return new ResetPasswordResponse(password);

171:         do {

173:             for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
174:                 builder.append(PASSWORD_CHARS.charAt(secureRandom.nextInt(PASSWORD_CHARS.length())));
175:             }
176:             candidate = builder.toString();
177:         } while (!passwordPolicyService.isValid(candidate));
178:         return candidate;

11: import java.security.SecureRandom;
12: import java.time.Duration;
13: import java.time.Instant;
14: import java.util.Base64;
15: import java.util.List;

17: import java.util.UUID;
18: 
19: @Service
20: @RequiredArgsConstructor
21: public class SessionService {
22: 
23:     public static final String SESSION_COOKIE = "SESSION_TOKEN";
24:     public static final String CSRF_COOKIE = "XSRF-TOKEN";
25: 
26:     private final UserSessionRepository userSessionRepository;
27:     private final AppProperties appProperties;
28:     private final SecureRandom secureRandom = new SecureRandom();
29: 
30:     @Transactional
31:     public UserSession createSession(User user, String ipAddress, String userAgent) {
32:         UserSession session = new UserSession();
33:         session.setId(UUID.randomUUID().toString());
34:         session.setUser(user);
35:         session.setCreatedAt(Instant.now());
36:         session.setLastActiveAt(Instant.now());
37:         session.setAbsoluteExpiry(Instant.now().plus(Duration.ofHours(appProperties.getSession().getAbsoluteTimeoutHours())));
38:         session.setValid(true);
39:         session.setIpAddress(ipAddress);
40:         session.setUserAgent(userAgent);
41:         session.setCsrfToken(generateCsrfToken());
42:         return userSessionRepository.save(session);
43:     }
44: 
45:     @Transactional(readOnly = true)
46:     public Optional<UserSession> findValidSession(String token) {
47:         if (token == null) {
48:             return Optional.empty();
49:         }
50:         return userSessionRepository.findById(token)
51:             .filter(UserSession::isValid)
52:             .filter(session -> session.getAbsoluteExpiry().isAfter(Instant.now()));
53:     }
54: 
55:     @Transactional
56:     public void touchSession(UserSession session) {
57:         session.setLastActiveAt(Instant.now());
58:         userSessionRepository.save(session);
59:     }
60: 
61:     @Transactional
62:     public void invalidateSession(UserSession session) {
63:         session.setValid(false);
64:         userSessionRepository.save(session);
65:     }
66: 
67:     @Transactional
68:     public void invalidateAllSessions(Long userId) {
69:         List<UserSession> sessions = userSessionRepository.findByUser_Id(userId);
70:         if (sessions.isEmpty()) {
71:             return;
72:         }
73:         sessions.stream()
74:             .filter(UserSession::isValid)
75:             .forEach(session -> session.setValid(false));
76:         userSessionRepository.saveAll(sessions);
77:     }
78: 
79:     private String generateCsrfToken() {
80:         byte[] bytes = new byte[32];
81:         secureRandom.nextBytes(bytes);
82:         return Base64.getUrlEncoder().withoutPadding().encodeToString(bytes);
83:     }
84: }

**Ensuring single logging and imports for StepUp**

Iâ€™m making sure the StepUp verification logs its result only once by adjusting how map and orElseGet handle logging. Iâ€™ll verify all necessary imports, like java.util.Optional and lombokâ€™s Slf4j, are in place and check that existing log statements across files meet the instructions. Though mvn compile isnâ€™t available to run, Iâ€™ll note that and confirm my log additions satisfy the userâ€™s requirements before summarizing test attempts.

- Added structured logging to the core services by annotating each with `@Slf4j` and inserting `log.info` statements around the requested operations (auth attempts, job lifecycle steps, review decisions, appeals processing, claim/ticket creation, admin user mutations, session handling, dashboard creation/export, and step-up verification) so key business events surface in logs (`backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/service/*.java`).
- No automated tests run; `cd backend && mvn compile -q` still fails here because `mvn` is not installedâ€”please rerun once Maven or the wrapper is available.

# Fix: Rate Limit Filter Should Read From Config

## Priority: LOW â€” Config values are misleading (hardcoded constants ignored)

## Files to modify
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/security/RateLimitFilter.java`
- `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/config/AppProperties.java`

## Problem

`RateLimitFilter` hardcodes `USER_LIMIT = 60` and `AUTH_LIMIT = 20` as constants. The `application.yml` value `app.rate-limit.per-minute: 60` is never read. Changing config has no effect â€” this is misleading.

## Step 1 â€” Add auth-per-minute to config

Open `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml`. Find:

```yaml
  rate-limit:
    per-minute: 60
```

Replace with:

```yaml
  rate-limit:
    per-minute: 60
    auth-per-minute: 20
```

## Step 2 â€” Add the field to AppProperties

Open `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/config/AppProperties.java`.

Find the `RateLimit` inner class. It should have a `perMinute` field. Add `authPerMinute` to it. The class should look like:

```java
    @Data
    public static class RateLimit {
        private int perMinute = 60;
        private int authPerMinute = 20;
    }
```

If `RateLimit` class doesn't exist, find the `rateLimit` field and add the inner class.

## Step 3 â€” Use config in RateLimitFilter

Open `backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/security/RateLimitFilter.java`.

Find these constants (lines 26-27):

```java
    private static final int AUTH_LIMIT = 20;
    private static final int USER_LIMIT = 60;
```

Remove both lines.

Then add injection of `AppProperties`. Find:

```java
    private final SessionService sessionService;
```

Add after it:

```java
    private final AppProperties appProperties;
```

Add the import at the top:

```java
import com.shiftworks.jobops.config.AppProperties;
```

Then in `doFilterInternal`, find:

```java
            if (!allowRequest(userBuckets, userId.toString(), USER_LIMIT)) {
```

Replace with:

```java
            if (!allowRequest(userBuckets, userId.toString(), appProperties.getRateLimit().getPerMinute())) {
```

And find:

```java
            if (!allowRequest(ipBuckets, key, AUTH_LIMIT)) {
```

Replace with:

```java
            if (!allowRequest(ipBuckets, key, appProperties.getRateLimit().getAuthPerMinute())) {
```

## Verification

After saving, confirm:
1. No hardcoded `USER_LIMIT` or `AUTH_LIMIT` constants remain in `RateLimitFilter.java`
2. The project compiles: `cd backend && mvn compile -q`

content.text:
<path>/home/leul/Documents/eaglepoint-task2/task-44/backend/src/main/java/com/shiftworks/jobops/security/RateLimitFilter.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package com.shiftworks.jobops.security;
2: 
3: import com.shiftworks.jobops.entity.UserSession;
4: import com.shiftworks.jobops.service.SessionService;
5: import com.shiftworks.jobops.util.CookieUtils;
6: import jakarta.servlet.FilterChain;
7: import jakarta.servlet.ServletException;
8: import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
9: import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;
10: import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
11: import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
12: import org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;
13: import org.springframework.stereotype.Component;
14: import org.springframework.web.filter.OncePerRequestFilter;
15: 
16: import java.io.IOException;
17: import java.time.Instant;
18: import java.util.Map;
19: import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
20: 
21: @Slf4j
22: @Component
23: @RequiredArgsConstructor
24: public class RateLimitFilter extends OncePerRequestFilter {
25: 
26:     private static final int AUTH_LIMIT = 20;
27:     private static final int USER_LIMIT = 60;
28: 
29:     private final SessionService sessionService;
30: 
31:     private final Map<String, BucketHolder> userBuckets = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
32:     private final Map<String, BucketHolder> ipBuckets = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
33: 
34:     @Override
35:     protected boolean shouldNotFilter(HttpServletRequest request) {
36:         return !request.getRequestURI().startsWith("/api/");
37:     }
38: 
39:     @Override
40:     protected void doFilterInternal(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response, FilterChain filterChain) throws ServletException, IOException {
41:         Long userId = resolveUserId(request);
42:         boolean isAuthEndpoint = request.getRequestURI().startsWith("/api/auth");
43:         if (userId != null) {
44:             if (!allowRequest(userBuckets, userId.toString(), USER_LIMIT)) {
45:                 writeRateLimitResponse(response);
46:                 return;
47:             }
48:         } else if (isAuthEndpoint) {
49:             String key = request.getRemoteAddr();
50:             if (!allowRequest(ipBuckets, key, AUTH_LIMIT)) {
51:                 writeRateLimitResponse(response);
52:                 return;
53:             }
54:         }
55:         filterChain.doFilter(request, response);
56:     }
57: 
58:     private boolean allowRequest(Map<String, BucketHolder> buckets, String key, int limit) {
59:         BucketHolder holder = buckets.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> new BucketHolder(new TokenBucket(limit, limit)));
60:         holder.touch();
61:         return holder.bucket.tryConsume();
62:     }
63: 
64:     private Long resolveUserId(HttpServletRequest request) {
65:         UserSession existing = (UserSession) request.getAttribute(SessionAuthFilter.SESSION_REQUEST_ATTRIBUTE);
66:         if (existing != null) {
67:             return existing.getUser().getId();
68:         }
69:         return CookieUtils.getCookie(request, SessionService.SESSION_COOKIE)
70:             .flatMap(sessionService::findValidSession)
71:             .map(session -> {
72:                 request.setAttribute(SessionAuthFilter.SESSION_REQUEST_ATTRIBUTE, session);
73:                 return session.getUser().getId();
74:             })
75:             .orElse(null);
76:     }
77: 
78:     private void writeRateLimitResponse(HttpServletResponse response) throws IOException {
79:         response.setStatus(429);
80:         response.setContentType("application/json");
81:         response.getWriter().write("{\"code\":429,\"message\":\"Rate limit exceeded\"}");
82:     }
83: 
84:     @Scheduled(fixedDelay = 300_000)
85:     public void cleanBuckets() {
86:         Instant cutoff = Instant.now().minusSeconds(600);
87:         userBuckets.entrySet().removeIf(entry -> entry.getValue().isStale(cutoff));
88:         ipBuckets.entrySet().removeIf(entry -> entry.getValue().isStale(cutoff));
89:     }
90: 
91:     private static final class BucketHolder {
92:         private final TokenBucket bucket;
93:         private volatile Instant lastTouched;
94: 
95:         private BucketHolder(TokenBucket bucket) {
96:             this.bucket = bucket;
97:             this.lastTouched = Instant.now();
98:         }
99: 
100:         private void touch() {
101:             this.lastTouched = Instant.now();
102:         }
103: 
104:         private boolean isStale(Instant cutoff) {
105:             return lastTouched.isBefore(cutoff);
106:         }
107:     }
108: }

(End of file - total 108 lines)
</content>

# Fix: Add Flat Pay Config to application.yml

## Priority: MEDIUM â€” Flat-rate job postings are broken (forced to $0)

## Files to modify
- `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml`

## Problem

`application.yml` defines `hourly-pay` bounds but has no `flat-pay` section. `AppProperties.Range` defaults `min` and `max` to `0` (Java `int` default). So `JobPostingService.validateBaseRequest()` calls `getFlatPay()` which returns `Range(0, 0)`, meaning any flat-rate pay amount > 0 fails validation.

## Instructions

Open `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml`.

Find this block (around line 45):

```yaml
    hourly-pay:
      min: 12
      max: 75
```

Add directly after it:

```yaml
    flat-pay:
      min: 12
      max: 5000
```

The final `job-validation` section should look like:

```yaml
  job-validation:
    validity-days:
      min: 30
      max: 90
    hourly-pay:
      min: 12
      max: 75
    flat-pay:
      min: 12
      max: 5000
    headcount:
      min: 1
      max: 500
    weekly-hours:
      min: 1
      max: 80
```

## Verification

After saving, confirm:
1. `grep -A2 'flat-pay' backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` shows `min: 12` and `max: 5000`
2. The project compiles: `cd backend && mvn compile -q`
